what_magic_is: Magic is a world-level force expressed through canon-bearing entities, ritual systems, divine structures, and setting-specific anomalies rather than a universal generic fantasy default.
where_common_or_rare: Visibility and social familiarity vary by polity, cultic context, and locality; absence of open use does not imply nonexistence.
cost_risk: Use, exposure, and contact may carry spiritual, legal, bodily, political, or social costs depending on context.
social_legitimacy: Legitimacy is mediated by local law, faith, class power, and public fear.
legal_restrictions: Magical practice must be interpreted through local authority and law packets rather than assumed globally lawful.
religious_interpretation: Many magical phenomena are filtered through divine, ritual, and doctrinal understanding.
daily_life_visibility: Ordinary life may include indirect magical consequences even where overt casting is uncommon.
regional_variation: Magic expression must vary by region, polity, cultic presence, and cosmological pressure.
justification: Repository synthesis layer built to make strongly supported canon operational without overriding source truth.
